title: elaphoglossum sherringii
intro: Elaphoglossum sherringii is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae, characterized by its unique, delicate fronds and creeping rhizome. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits associated with Elaphoglossum sherringii include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by infusing the leaves in hot water or alcohol to create a tea or tincture, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
